title: Pulmonary Vascular Development and the Neonatal Circulation

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Pulmonary Vascular Development?
Pulmonary vasculature ไม่ใช่แค่ท่อ — มันเป็น active, developmentally programmed organ ที่ต้องเปลี่ยนสภาพอย่างรวดเร็วในชั่วโมงแรกหลังคลอด. (1) PPHN คือ failure of transition — PVR ไม่ลดลงตามที่ควร → right-to-left shunting → profound hypoxemia. (2) BPD-PH คือ arrested vascular development ตาม arrested alveolarization — BPD ที่มี PH มี mortality สูงกว่า BPD ปกติหลายเท่า. (3) NO-cGMP pathway คือ core vasodilator mechanism ที่ peak ที่ term — เหตุผลที่ iNO ได้ผลดีใน term PPHN แต่ response ไม่แน่นอนใน extremely preterm. (4) CDH = most refractory PH in neonates — leading indication for ECMO, vascular hypoplasia + remodeling superimposed. (5) SSRIs + NSAIDs ในแม่ → ductal constriction → PPHN — ประวัติยาแม่สำคัญมากใน workup PPHN. เข้าใจ pathway → เข้าใจ rationale ของ iNO, sildenafil, ET antagonist, milrinone.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย fetal pulmonary circulation: PVR สูง, PaO2 ~18 mmHg, เปอร์เซ็นต์ cardiac output ที่ไปปอด
อธิบาย role ของ HIF-1α, VEGF, eNOS/NO/sGC/cGMP pathway ใน fetal pulmonary vascular development
อธิบาย mediators ของ pulmonary vascular function: NO, PGI2, ET-1, serotonin, PDE5
อธิบาย mechanism การเปลี่ยนแปลงที่ birth: ventilation + O2 → 8-fold ↑ pulmonary blood flow
รู้ทำไม sGC peak ที่ term และ PDE5 peak ใน newborn period — clinical implication ต่อ iNO response
อธิบาย histologic features ของ PPHN vs BPD-PH (vascular remodeling patterns)
รู้ factors ที่ disrupt fetal PVD: genetic (CRHR1, Down syndrome/endostatin, BMPR2), ductal closure, placental insufficiency, oligohydramnios, NSAIDs, SSRIs, CDH, ACD
อธิบาย CDH pathophysiology: ทำไม PH refractory, บทบาท LV hypoplasia
รู้ ACD: Foxf1 mutations, misaligned veins, fatal outcome, บทบาท vascular shunts
เชื่อมกับ NICU: เมื่อไหร่ใช้ iNO, sildenafil, ระวัง SSRI/NSAID exposure, target O2 ใน PPHN vs preterm PH
1. Fetal Pulmonary Circulation — ทำไม PVR สูงโดย Design
Pulmonary hypertension คือ physiologic normal state ใน fetal life — เพราะ gas exchange เกิดที่ placenta ไม่ใช่ปอด. PVR สูงเพื่อ redirect RV output ผ่าน ductus arteriosus ไป descending aorta
1.1 Key Hemodynamic Values ใน Fetal Lamb

rows: ปอดอยู่ในสภาวะ hypoxia ปกติ → HIF active → VEGF ↑ → vascular growth
16% combined ventricular output (13% ใน human at 20 wk)
ส่วนใหญ่ผ่าน DA → aorta
Oxygenated blood returns from placenta (analog of pulmonary vein)
Hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction + liquid-filled alveoli + ↓ cross-sectional area
เมื่อ lung expand + O2 ↑ → PVR ↓ dramatically

blocks.text: 1.2 Developmental Changes in Reactivity

Early second trimester (13–20 wk, canalicular): pulmonary vasculature ยังไม่ respond to O2 — maternal hyperoxia ไม่ทำให้ pulmonary blood flow ↑. นี่เป็นเหตุผลที่ GA 23–26 wk birth มีความเสี่ยง PPHN ~2% (และ iNO use 6–8%)

Third trimester (saccular stage): vessels ↑ cross-sectional area + ↑ reactivity → สามารถ vasodilate ต่อ O2 ได้ → maternal hyperoxygenation test (60% O2 by face mask) ใช้ predict pulmonary reactivity ใน CDH

Near term: PVR very sensitive to small ΔPO2. Konduri 1993: ↑ PA PaO2 7 mm Hg → 3-fold ↑ pulmonary blood flow ใน fetal lambs. Arraut 2013: maternal hypoxia (12% O2) → ↑ PA pulsatility index 5-fold (vasoconstriction)

Pulmonary Veins: Reactive Vessels ที่มักถูกมองข้าม
Pulmonary veins ไม่ใช่แค่ passive conduits — veins ใน fetus contribute significantly to total PVR and are primary sites of action ของ vasoconstrictors: endothelin + thromboxane. At birth: veins dilate ต่อ NO + PGI2 (oxygen-dependent, via protein kinase G). Pulmonary venous hypertension (เช่น ใน CDH จาก LV hypoplasia) → diminishes iNO response ช่วงแรก. อย่าลืม assess LV function เมื่อ iNO ไม่ตอบสนองใน CDH.
2. Mediators of Fetal Pulmonary Vascular Development
2.1 HIF-1α — Master Regulator ใน Hypoxic Environment
HIFs = hypoxia-inducible factors: heterodimers of O2-sensitive α-subunit (HIF-1α, HIF-2α) + constitutive β-subunit
Hypoxia → HIF-1α stabilized (prolyl hydroxylase inactivated) → nuclear translocation → activate target genes: VEGF, erythropoietin, glycolytic enzymes
Normoxia/hyperoxia → prolyl hydroxylase active → HIF-1α hydroxylated → ubiquitinated → proteosomal degradation → ↓ VEGF → disrupted vascular development

HIF-1α deletion → embryonic lethal; HIF-2α deletion → ↓ VEGF → early death from respiratory failure (Compernolle Nat Med 2002). fetal hypoxia IS normal — required stimulus for lung + vascular development

Preterm lamb hyperoxia: brief hyperoxic ventilation → ↑ prolyl hydroxylase → rapidly degrades HIF-1α/2α → ↓ downstream VEGF → abnormal vascular development (Grover Am J Physiol 2007). เหตุผลที่ต้อง avoid unnecessary hyperoxia ตั้งแต่ DR
2.2 VEGF — Central Angiogenic Driver
VEGF: produced by distal airway epithelial cells; receptors VEGFR-1 (Flt-1) + VEGFR-2 (Flk-1) on pulmonary endothelial cells. VEGF transcription regulated by HIF → ↑ in hypoxic fetal conditions

Individual knockout Vegf, Vegfr1, Vegfr2 → embryonic lethal with disorganized endothelium. VEGFR inhibitor SU5416: impairs alveolar development → BPD-like pathology; even in adult rats → PH + emphysema (requires VEGF for maintenance, not just formation)

VEGF-induced vasodilation: mediated partly via eNOS — VEGF inhibition → ↓ eNOS → ↓ NO. But in eNOS-deficient mice, recombinant VEGF still restores lung structure after hyperoxia → VEGF has eNOS-independent mechanisms too
2.3 eNOS/NO/sGC/cGMP Axis
eNOS mRNA + protein: present early fetal life in rats + sheep, ↑ with advancing gestation. Regulated by: hemodynamic forces, estradiol, VEGF, O2 tension, substrate (L-arginine) availability

eNOS-deficient mice: ↓ alveolarization + ↓ vascularization + more susceptible to hypoxia-induced vascular damage (Han Circ Res 2004). Can still make birth transition (compensatory mechanisms: other NOS isoforms + PGI2) BUT → PH with mild ↓ PaO2 + ↑ neonatal mortality with hypoxia

sGC (soluble guanylate cyclase): main NO target in smooth muscle; sGC mRNA low ใน early preterm, ↑ markedly toward end of 3rd trimester (Mensah 1998). นี่อธิบายว่าทำไม iNO response ไม่แน่นอนใน extremely preterm infants

PDE5 (phosphodiesterase 5): breaks down cGMP; expression + activity peaks ใน immediate newborn period — creates capacity for fine-tuning cGMP at transition. Hyperoxia + ROS → ↑ PDE5 activity (via mitochondrial ROS) → ↓ cGMP → vasoconstriction. Sildenafil (PDE5 inhibitor) blocks this → vasodilation
2.4 Other Transcription Factors

Foxf1 (Forkhead box f1): regulates vascular cell proliferation/differentiation. Foxf1 haploinsufficiency = 40% of ACD (alveolar capillary dysplasia) — lethal neonatal PH syndrome. Endothelial-specific Foxf1 deletion → embryonic lethal with lung + placenta + retinal vascular defects

NF-κB: constitutive expression higher in neonatal than adult lung → protective in neonatal lung development (contrasts with its inflammatory role elsewhere). Inhibiting NF-κB during alveolar stage → alveolar simplification + ↓ capillary density (regulated via VEGFR-2)

Endothelial progenitor cells (EPCs / ECFCs): resident microvascular EPCs (express CD31, CD144, eNOS, vWF, CD34, CD309) — highly proliferative. Human fetal lung ECFCs: hyperoxia ↓ proliferation + ↓ capillary-like networks. Cord blood–derived ECFCs → restore alveolar/vascular growth in hyperoxic rodents (paracrine mechanism)
3. Mediators of Fetal Pulmonary Vascular Function
3.1 NO/cGMP — Primary Vasodilator Pathway

VEGF → acute NO release → rapid pulmonary vasodilation in vivo. Chronic VEGFR inhibition → ↓ eNOS → ↑ PVR ใน late-gestation fetus. eNOS inhibitor (L-NAME) → ↑ basal PVR ตั้งแต่ 75% gestation → endogenous NOS contributes to vasoregulation in late gestation
NO response (endothelium-independent) precedes endothelium-dependent responses (acetylcholine, O2) developmentally — important for timing of vasodilator therapies
3.2 Prostacyclin (PGI2) — Secondary Vasodilator
PGI2: produced by pulmonary endothelial cells → IP receptor on SMC → ↑ cAMP → vasodilation. cAMP inactivated by PDE3A. Rhythmic lung distention + shear stress → ↑ PGI2
Overall: PGI2 plays less important role than NO in regulating fetal + transitional pulmonary vascular tone, but provides compensation when eNOS is absent
3.3 Vasoconstrictors — ET-1, Thromboxane, Serotonin

rows: ET-A on SMC (constriction); ET-B on EC (→ NO → dilation)
Primary vasoconstrictor ใน fetal circulation. ↑ ใน PPHN + CDH. Bosentan (dual ET-A/B antagonist), macitentan ใช้ใน chronic PH
Group B strep model → ↑ TxA2. Pulmonary veins are primary site. Less role in normal fetal PVR
↑ PVR in fetus. SSRIs (sertraline, fluoxetine) → ↑ PVR acutely. SSRIs in late pregnancy → PPHN risk (Chambers NEJM 2006)
Contribute to fetal PVR, role in transition unclear. Leukotrienes elevated in MAS, contribute to vasoconstriction

blocks.text:
SSRI ในแม่ → PPHN: Mechanism + ขนาดความเสี่ยง
Mechanism: SSRIs → ↑ serotonin → 5-HT2A + Rho-kinase → ↑ PVR; AND constrict ductus arteriosus → ↑ pulmonary arterial pressure (Hooper Am J Physiol 2016). Epidemiology: Chambers NEJM 2006: SSRI late pregnancy → ↑ PPHN risk; แต่ absolute risk modest เมื่อ control for maternal depression (Huybrechts JAMA 2015). Practice: ไม่ใช่ contraindication สำหรับ SSRI ใน maternal depression — แต่ ประวัติ SSRI use ในแม่ต้องอยู่ใน PPHN workup เสมอ.
4. Transitional Circulation — The Birth Switch

Birth = ชุด circulatory events ที่ต้องเกิดพร้อมกัน: pulmonary blood flow ↑ 8-fold, PVR ↓ drammatically, foramen ovale closes functionally, DA begins constriction. Most important stimuli: ventilation + ↑ oxygen tension
4.1 Mechanisms ของ Pulmonary Vasodilation at Birth

NO (endothelial): pulmonary endothelial NO production ↑ markedly at birth. NOS inhibitor (L-NAME) attenuates PVR fall → NO responsible for ~50% of rise in pulmonary blood flow at birth. O2 is key catalyst: maternal hyperoxia in near-term fetal lamb → PA PaO2 19→48 mmHg + pulmonary blood flow 34→298 mL/kg/min

Oxygen direct effect: both direct vasoactive + via stimulating NO production. 100% O2 resuscitation vs 21% O2: ↑ rate of PVR fall slightly in first minutes but PVR equalizes within minutes AND 100% O2 → ROS → impairs subsequent NO relaxation — Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Res 2007

sGC + PDE5: sGC expression peaks in late gestation (explains why iNO response > in neonates than any other age group). PDE5 peaks ใน immediate newborn period → fine-tuning cGMP. Together = maximal cGMP regulatory capacity at birth

PGI2: rhythmic distention + shear stress → ↑ PGI2 + NO at birth. Role important but less than NO. Cord clamping: removes low-resistance placental circuit → ↑ systemic pressure as PA pressure falls → drives functional FO closure
4.2 Preterm Transition — Slower and Incomplete

In premature lambs (70% gestation): similar vasodilator mechanisms (NO + rhythmic distension) BUT pulmonary arterial pressure decrease is significantly slower than term (Randala Acta Paediatr 1996). PA pressure elevation may persist days in extremely preterm infants

40% of extremely preterm infants: echocardiographic markers of pulmonary vascular dysfunction in week 1 → strong risk factor for BPD + late PH (Mourani AJRCCM 2015). Not "natural high PVR" protecting from ductal steal — it is abnormal vascular development

100% O2 ใน DR — ทำไมต้องระวัง
100% O2 ใน resuscitation → ↑ ROS (hydrogen peroxide, superoxide, peroxynitrite) → impairs NO relaxation + ↓ eNOS expression + ↑ PDE5 activity + ↓ cGMP. ผล: vasoconstriction + vascular remodeling คล้าย PPHN (Brennan Circ Res 2003). NRP 2021: preterm: เริ่ม 21% O2; term/near-term: 21% O2 หรือ air. ใน PPHN established: titrate O2 to maintain SpO2 95–98% (ไม่ใช่ 100%).
5. Features of Abnormal Pulmonary Vascular Development
5.1 PPHN Histology — Term Infant

Vascular remodeling: muscularization of smallest arteries (< 30 μm external diameter) at level of alveolar duct and wall; ↑ medial wall thickness of intraacinar arteries (2-fold). Suggests structural maldevelopment begins in utero, not just failure of fetal pattern to regress

Adventitial thickening → ↑ PA stiffness. Adventitial cells (fibroblasts, pericytes, progenitor cells) regulate vascular wall from "outside in" — constitutive NADPH oxidase → superoxide → scavenges NO → ↓ NO efficacy on adventitial side

Signaling abnormalities: ↓ eNOS expression + ↓ NO metabolites + ↓ sGC expression/activity (partly from oxidation making it NO-insensitive) + ↑ PDE5 activity + ↓ prostacyclin synthase. ↑ ET-1 circulating + lung levels → ET-A → RhoA-ROCK pathway → phospho-MLCK → ↑ intracellular Ca²⁺ → vasoconstriction + SMC proliferation
5.2 BPD-PH Histology — Preterm Infant

Contrasts with term PPHN: BPD = arrested development (vascular pruning + ↓ alveoli) not just remodeling. Histology: ↓ numbers of alveoli + intraacinar arteries, ↓ vascular branching, altered vascular distribution

Intrapulmonary bronchopulmonary anastomoses (AV shunts): recently identified in severe BPD — contribute to hypoxemia; may represent compensatory "pop-off" to protect RV or compensate for ↓ vascular surface area (Galambos Ann Am Thorac Soc 2013)

Similar to PPHN: smooth muscle proliferation extends abnormally into peripheral arteries. 10–14% of extremely preterm infants → chronic PH at 36 wk PMA. Risk factors: severity of BPD, fetal growth restriction, oligohydramnios

Pulmonary Vein Stenosis (PVS) — BPD Complication ที่ Aggressive
PVS: rare complication of severe BPD; left-sided veins > right; stenosis of even single vein → severe progressive PH. Median age diagnosis: 6 months; not hereditary (no concordance in twins); associated with NEC. Mortality 30–50% within 2 years after diagnosis. Mechanisms unknown. Treat with balloon dilation/stenting — often temporizing. Suspect PVS in severe BPD + disproportionate PH refractory to vasodilators, especially left-sided.
6. Factors That Disrupt Fetal Pulmonary Vascular Development
6.1 Genetic Factors
BMPR2, CAV1, KCNK3, EIF2AK4: mutations identified in adults with PH — few found in neonates with PPHN

CRHR1 polymorphisms: higher rates in neonates with PPHN + evidence of functional adrenal insufficiency (Byers Pediatr Res 2012). CRHR1 near PPAR-γ binding site — PPAR-γ regulates PA smooth muscle proliferation + vascular tone

CPS1 (carbamoyl-phosphate synthetase-1) polymorphism: rate-limiting enzyme of urea cycle → L-arginine → eNOS substrate. CPS1 polymorphism → ↓ plasma arginine → ↓ NO metabolites → ↑ PPHN risk (Pearson NEJM 2001;344:1832)

Down syndrome (Trisomy 21): 10-fold ↑ risk of idiopathic PPHN. Dutch cohort: PPHN ใน 5.2% ของ Down syndrome ที่ไม่มี cardiac disease. More severe PH with CHD than euploid infants. Mechanism: chromosome 21 carries antiangiogenic genes — endostatin (↑ 3-fold mRNA ใน prenatal Down lung) → ↓ VEGF signaling → ↓ microvascular density + ↑ thickened PA walls + persistent double capillary network
6.2 Antenatal Ductal Closure

DA critical for fetal circulation: directs RV output to aorta + protects pulmonary circulation from volume overload. Ductal ligation in fetal lamb → ↑ PA pressure → endothelial dysfunction → poor response to O2 + acetylcholine → ↓ eNOS → ↓ sGC + ↑ PDE5 (within days) → severe PPHN at birth

NSAIDs (COX inhibitors): prostaglandins maintain ductal patency. NSAIDs → ductal constriction/closure → pulmonary vascular remodeling. Analysis of meconium from PPHN infants: NSAIDs ใน ~50% (Alano Pediatrics 2001). Aspirin in 3rd trimester → ↑ PPHN risk (Van Marter Pediatrics 2013). Ibuprofen at any gestation → no significant PPHN association
6.3 Placental Insufficiency

FGR + absent end-diastolic flow → right-to-left shunting across FO + DA. Preterm FGR associated with BPD-PH later: cord blood ↓ placental growth factor + ↓ VEGF-A → predict subsequent PH (Mestan J Pediatr 2017)

Placental pathology: maternal vascular underperfusion (MVU) + decreased villous vascularity → failed placental angiogenesis → global vascular signaling abnormalities → postnatal PH (Mestan Placenta 2014; Yallapragada Pediatr Dev Pathol 2016). Chorioamnionitis + preeclampsia + maternal smoking → also impair lung vascular development
6.4 Oligohydramnios + Prolonged PROM

Preterm infants with PROM + oligohydramnios + some pulmonary hypoplasia → severe early PH with ↓ tracheal aspirate nitrates/nitrites → specific NO deficiency. Prompt response to iNO (Ball J Pediatr 2012; Aikio J Pediatr 2012)

Mechanism: oligohydramnios → ↓ lung liquid volume + ↓ fetal breathing → ↓ stretch stimuli → reduced vascular growth factors → ↓ arteriole density + ↑ muscular wall thickness (Thibeault Am J Perinatol 1997). Suggests amniotic fluid maintains vascular growth factor levels
6.5 Maternal Drug Exposures Summary

rows: ↓ PGE2 → ductal constriction/closure → pulmonary vascular remodeling
Aspirin 3rd trimester → ↑ PPHN risk (Van Marter 2013). NSAIDs found in meconium of ~50% PPHN infants. Ibuprofen: no association
Chambers NEJM 2006: modest PPHN risk. Huybrechts JAMA 2015: risk attenuated when controlling depression. Not absolute contraindication — weigh against depression risk
↓ oxidative stress → ↑ response to NO donors (Chandrasekar 2008)
Protective; ↓ PPHN possibly partly explains benefit of late preterm steroids (Gyamfi-Bannerman NEJM 2016)
Maternal sildenafil in nitrofen-CDH rat → ↓ vascular remodeling + ↓ RVH (Luong Circulation 2011). Under investigation for severe FGR

blocks.text: 7. Congenital Diaphragmatic Hernia (CDH) — Most Refractory Neonatal PH

CDH: 1 in 2500–3000 pregnancies; 8% of all major congenital anomalies; 80% left-sided. Leading neonatal respiratory indication for ECMO. Severe CDH = interrupted lung development = bilateral arrest of airway + vascular branching
7.1 Pathophysiology ของ CDH PH

NOT just compression alone — space-occupying lesions without CDH cause less vascular damage. Evidence: ↓ pulmonary blood flow alone sufficient to cause lung hypoplasia; genetic/environmental factors act before diaphragm development to arrest lung parenchyma + vasculature development bilaterally

Vascular pathology: ↓ cross-sectional area + thickened media + adventitia of small arterioles + abnormal muscularization extending to acinar arterioles (vascular hypoplasia + remodeling). Result: suprasystemic PVR + right-to-left shunting across FO + DA → profound hypoxemia

LV hypoplasia + dysfunction: key underappreciated component. LV + LA + septum hypoplastic relative to age-matched controls (from ↓ pulmonary venous return + RV compression). LV dysfunction → ↑ pulmonary venous pressure → pulmonary venous hypertension → ↓ iNO response early → explains why early CDH iNO trials failed to ↓ ECMO use

ET-1: marker of chronic PH in CDH; infants with CDH + poor outcomes have higher ET-1 at 2 weeks vs those discharged on room air (Keller AJRCCM 2010). ET-1 → ET-A → RhoA-ROCK → ↑ vascular tone + impaired angiogenesis

iNO in CDH: no mortality benefit in early trials, but useful for assess vasoreactivity + bridge to ECMO. Maternal hyperoxygenation test (60% O2 face mask) in 3rd trimester → pulmonary vasodilation response → predict postnatal survival in severe CDH (Done UOG 2011)

CDH Management Pearls: Think Biventricular, Not Just RV
CDH PH มีทั้ง (1) pulmonary arterial hypertension (↑ PVR → RV afterload) AND (2) pulmonary venous hypertension (LV hypoplasia/dysfunction). iNO → vasodilate pulmonary arteries → ↑ blood flow → ↑ LV preload → ถ้า LV cannot handle → ↑ LA/PV pressure → ↓ response or pulmonary edema. Assess LV echo ก่อน escalate vasodilators. Infants ที่ LV function poor อาจต้อง DA open (PDA = LV pop-off). Sildenafil + bosentan: second-line ใน CDH PH ที่ไม่ respond to iNO.
8. Alveolar Capillary Dysplasia (ACD) — The Fatal Mimic
ACD with misalignment of pulmonary veins: rare, universally fatal without transplant. Presents: severe PH + refractory hypoxemia ใน first days (occasional delayed presentation at months of life)

Histology: (1) remodeled pulmonary arterioles; (2) alveolar simplification; (3) "misaligned" pulmonary veins in same adventitial sheath as arteries + bronchi — actually bronchopulmonary anastomoses bypassing alveolar capillary bed (Galambos Thorax 2015)
FOXF1 mutations/deletions: found in 40% of ACD infants (Stankiewicz AJHG 2009). Other anomalies (GI, GU, CV) in ~50% — all explainable by aberrant Foxf1 signaling

No survivors without transplant; limited survival after bilateral lung transplant reported (Towe J Pediatr 2017). Suspect ACD in term infant with refractory PPHN + no response to maximal vasodilator therapy + echocardiographic evidence of RV hypertension. Lung biopsy confirms.
9. Factors That Disrupt Postnatal Pulmonary Vascular Development
9.1 Perinatal Asphyxia

Multiple mechanisms: fetal hypoxemia + ischemia + MAS + LV dysfunction + acidosis → ↑ PVR. pH < 7.25 → exaggerated hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction. Acute asphyxia = reversible vasoconstriction; chronic in utero asphyxia → vascular remodeling (Murphy J Pediatr 1984)

PH complicates ~25% of perinatal asphyxia cases. Therapeutic hypothermia (33.5°C): modest effect on PVR — does not significantly increase PPHN incidence (Thoresen J Pediatr 2011). Severe hypothermia (30°C): ↑ mean PA pressure 30% in lambs
9.2 Hypoxia

Chronic hypoxia → ↑ HIF activity → vascular remodeling + sustained vasoconstriction. HIF-1 activity stretch-inducible in fetal pulmonary vessels → myogenic response + vasoconstriction in PPHN vessels dramatically enhanced under hypoxia

NOS uncoupling: hypoxia → ↓ L-arginine availability → eNOS uncoupled → produces superoxide instead of NO → impairs NO signaling + increases oxidant stress. L-citrulline (arginine precursor) → rescue treatment in hypoxic newborn pig PH model (Fike Am J Respir Cell Mol Biol 2015)
9.3 Hyperoxia

Even brief hyperoxic ventilation (24 hours) → PH + RV hypertrophy persisting to day 14 in newborn rodents; associated with ↓ sGC + ↑ cGMP phosphodiesterase activity (Lee Am J Respir Cell Mol Biol 2014)

Mechanisms: hyperoxia → ROS (from immature antioxidant defense + NADPH oxidase) → ↓ eNOS expression + ↓ sGC activity + ↑ PDE5 → ↓ cGMP. Also: ↑ prolyl hydroxylase → ↓ HIF-1α/2α → ↓ VEGF → disrupted vascular development (Grover Am J Physiol 2007)

Nutritional deficiency amplifies hyperoxia effect: postnatal growth restriction → ↓ VEGFR + ↓ NOS → augments O2-induced PH (Wedgwood Pediatr Res 2016). ดังนั้น: เริ่ม PN วันแรก + เป้าหมาย protein 3–4 g/kg/day ช่วย vascular development ด้วย
10. NICU Practical — จาก Vascular Biology ถึง Bedside

PPHN work-up: ประวัติสำคัญ: maternal NSAID use (aspirin 3rd tri), SSRI exposure, preeclampsia, FGR, oligohydramnios, post-dates, MAS, Group B strep. Echo: assess RV function, PA pressure, LV size/function, FO/DA shunting direction, pulmonary venous return

iNO ใน term PPHN: 20 ppm, titrate down when improving. Response ดีเมื่อ: vasoreactive PH (OI > 25, not CDH/ACD). Response poor เมื่อ: CDH + LV dysfunction, ACD, pulmonary vein stenosis. ถ้า iNO ไม่ตอบสนองใน CDH → ประเมิน LV function + consider DA management

Sildenafil: PDE5 inhibitor → ↑ cGMP → vasodilation. Oral sildenafil ใน BPD-PH: start 0.5 mg/kg/dose q8h titrate. IV sildenafil (STARTS trial): ↑ mortality ใน older children — ห้ามใช้ IV sildenafil ใน PPHN. Oral/NG route acceptable
Oxygen targeting: PPHN → target SpO2 95–98%. Avoid 100% O2 prolonged — ↑ ROS → paradoxical vasoconstriction. Hypoxia < 91% → ↑ HIF → vasoconstriction + remodeling. Preterm PH: target 91–95%

Milrinone: PDE3 inhibitor → ↑ cAMP (inodilator). Useful in CDH/PPHN with LV dysfunction — improves LV function + pulmonary vasodilation via cAMP pathway. Lakshminrusimha Pediatr Crit Care Med 2009: milrinone → ↑ relaxation to PGI2 analog ใน PPHN lambs

Bosentan (ET-A/B antagonist): oral, used in BPD-PH + CDH chronic PH. ET-B block may prevent vasodilation-rebound — dual antagonist controversial. Macitentan: newer dual antagonist, under study in pediatric PH
Avoid triggers of PH crisis: agitation + suctioning + pain → ↑ SNS → ET-1 + serotonin release → acute PVR spike. Sedation + minimal stimulation + pre-medicate before procedures ใน severe PPHN
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — PPHN ≠ Single Entity: Vasoreactive vs Fixed vs Venous
PPHN มี 3 phenotypes ที่ treat ต่างกัน: (1) Vasoreactive (MAS, sepsis, asphyxia): responds to iNO, often self-limited. (2) Fixed vascular remodeling (CDH, chronic in utero stress): iNO partial; often needs ECMO + combined vasodilators. (3) Venous hypertension (LV hypoplasia/CDH, PVS): vasodilators may worsen pulmonary edema. Echo ก่อน escalate iNO เสมอ.

PEARL 2 — sGC Matures at Term — iNO ใน Extremely Preterm ≠ Reliable
sGC mRNA low in early preterm, peaks at term. นี่คือ biological explanation ว่าทำไม iNO ใน < 28 wk มี inconsistent response. Trial: INNOVO, Mercier trials → no mortality benefit of iNO in extremely preterm BPD prevention. FACT trial (Kinsella 2006): iNO ↓ BPD ใน 24–34 wk แต่ effect modified by severity. ปัจจุบัน: iNO ใน preterm → specific indication (PPHN with documented vasoreactivity, oligohydramnios PH) ไม่ใช่ routine BPD prevention.

PEARL 3 — Down Syndrome: Prepare for More Severe PH
Trisomy 21: 10-fold ↑ idiopathic PPHN; worse PH with CHD than euploid. Mechanism: ↑ endostatin (antiangiogenic) → ↓ VEGF signaling → ↓ microvascular density + ↑ PA wall thickness + persistent double capillary. 85% of Down syndrome autopsy → pulmonary vascular remodeling (Bush J Pediatr 2017). Practice: Down syndrome neonate → echo ก่อน discharge; lower threshold สำหรับ PPHN workup; expect longer course on vasodilators.

PEARL 4 — ACD: Never Give Up Before Biopsy
Refractory PPHN ใน term infant ที่ไม่ respond ต่อ iNO + sildenafil + ECMO อาจเป็น ACD. Red flags: (1) term, (2) no response to maximal therapy, (3) transient improvement then relapse, (4) ± associated anomalies (GI, GU). Lung biopsy (open or VATS) is the only way to confirm. ถ้า confirm ACD → counseling for bilateral lung transplantation. Foxf1 mutation testing supports diagnosis. No survivors without transplant.

PEARL 5 — Placental Pathology Predicts PH: Always Send the Placenta
FGR + BPD → placental pathology of maternal vascular underperfusion (MVU) → ↓ villous vascularity → ↓ cord blood VEGF-A + PlGF → predicts postnatal PH (Mestan J Pediatr 2017). ส่ง placenta pathology ทุก case ที่มี FGR + preterm. นอกจากนี้: cord blood angiogenic markers — ↓ VEGF-A ในเลือด cord → future biomarker panel for early PH risk stratification. เชื่อม vascular biology กับ prenatal surveillance.
12. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Steinhorn RH. The Newborn Lung 2019 (Ch3): Central reference — comprehensive review of fetal + neonatal pulmonary vascular development, mediators, and clinical conditions.
Pearson DL et al. NEJM 2001;344:1832: CPS1 polymorphism → ↓ arginine → ↓ NO metabolites → ↑ PPHN risk; established urea cycle as regulator of pulmonary vascular tone.
Chambers CD et al. NEJM 2006;354:579: SSRI late pregnancy → ↑ PPHN risk; landmark study that changed prescribing behavior for SSRIs near delivery.
Huybrechts KF et al. JAMA 2015;313:2142: SSRI + PPHN risk attenuated when controlling for maternal depression; absolute risk modest — SSRI not absolutely contraindicated.

Compernolle V et al. Nat Med 2002;8:702: HIF-2α deletion → ↓ VEGF → fatal respiratory distress in premature mice; VEGF treatment prevents respiratory failure — established VEGF-HIF axis in perinatal lung.
Mourani PM et al. Am J Respir Crit Care Med 2015;191:87: Early PH in 40% of extremely preterm in week 1; strong risk factor for BPD and late PH — validated early echo for PH risk stratification.
Mestan KK et al. J Pediatr 2017;185:33: Cord blood VEGF-A ↓ in placental MVU → predicts BPD-PH; links prenatal placental pathology to postnatal vascular disease.
Lakshminrusimha S et al. Pediatr Res 2007;62:313: 21% vs 50% vs 100% O2 resuscitation in neonatal lambs — 100% O2 → ↑ ROS → impaired subsequent NO relaxation; supports low-O2 initiation in DR.
Galambos C et al. Ann Am Thorac Soc 2013;10:474: 3D reconstruction identifying intrapulmonary AV anastomoses in severe BPD — explains hypoxemia disproportionate to parenchymal disease.
Lee KJ et al. Am J Respir Cell Mol Biol 2014;50:369: 24 hours hyperoxia → disrupted cGMP signaling → PH persisting to day 14 in newborn mice; supports aggressive O2 restriction from birth.
Bush D et al. J Pediatr 2017;180:156: 85% of Down syndrome autopsy → pulmonary vascular remodeling + AV anastomoses; underscores high PH prevalence in Trisomy 21.
Stankiewicz P et al. AJHG 2009;84:780: FOXF1 deletions/mutations in 40% of ACD — established Foxf1 as causative gene and enabled genetic testing for ACD diagnosis.
Gyamfi-Bannerman C et al. NEJM 2016;374:1311 (ALPS trial): Antenatal betamethasone for late preterm 34–36 wk → ↓ respiratory morbidity; reduced PPHN partly explains benefit.
Fike CD et al. Am J Respir Cell Mol Biol 2015;53:255: L-citrulline rescues hypoxia-induced PH in newborn pigs — supports NOS substrate augmentation as therapeutic strategy.
13. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: ทำไม fetal pulmonary vascular resistance สูง? อะไรคือ physiologic rationale?

ตอบ: PH คือ physiologic normal state ใน fetal life เพราะ gas exchange อยู่ที่ placenta. High PVR → RV output ผ่าน DA → descending aorta → placenta (oxygenation). ถ้า PVR ไม่สูง → blood flow to lung → ↑ LA return → ↑ LV output → underutilize placenta. Mechanisms: (1) hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction (PaO2 ~18 mmHg); (2) liquid-filled alveoli compress vessels; (3) ↓ cross-sectional vascular area (ยังพัฒนาไม่เต็มที่); (4) vasoconstrictors: ET-1, thromboxane, serotonin active. Only 16% of combined ventricular output reaches lung in fetus (13% ใน human at 20 wk).
Q: อธิบาย HIF-1α pathway ใน fetal lung. ทำไม hyperoxia ถึง disrupt vascular development?

ตอบ: Hypoxia (normal fetal state) → stabilizes HIF-1α → dimerizes with HIF-1β → nucleus → binds HRE → activates VEGF + EPO + glycolytic enzymes → angiogenesis + alveologenesis via VEGF → NO → sGC → cGMP. Hyperoxia: prolyl hydroxylase active → hydroxylates HIF-1α → ubiquitinated → proteosomal degradation → ↓ VEGF → disrupted vascular development. Specifically: brief hyperoxia → ↑ prolyl hydroxylase → degrades HIF-1α/2α → ↓ VEGF (Grover 2007). Also: hyperoxia → ROS → ↓ eNOS + ↓ sGC + ↑ PDE5 → ↓ cGMP → vasoconstriction. Implication: even brief 100% O2 in DR → ↑ ROS → impaired subsequent vasodilation.
Q: ทำไม iNO ได้ผลดีใน term PPHN แต่ inconsistent ใน < 28 wk preterm?

ตอบ: sGC (main target of NO in SMC): mRNA levels low during early preterm gestation, ↑ markedly toward term (Mensah 1998). นอกจากนี้ ↓ eNOS activity ใน preterm → ↓ basal NO. เมื่อ sGC ต่ำ → iNO cannot produce adequate cGMP → poor vasodilation. Term infant: sGC peaks → maximal cGMP response → iNO very effective. Clinical: iNO ใน < 28 wk มี variable response; INNOVO/Mercier trials: no BPD prevention benefit. Use iNO preterm only with documented vasoreactivity (OI > 25) หรือ specific indication (oligohydramnios-PH).
Q: CDH พัฒนา pulmonary hypertension ได้อย่างไร? ทำไม LV assessment สำคัญ?

ตอบ: CDH → bilateral arrest of airway + vascular branching → ↓ cross-sectional PA area + vascular remodeling (thickened media + adventitia + abnormal muscularization). At birth: suprasystemic PVR → right-to-left shunting FO + DA → severe hypoxemia. NOT just compression alone — genetic/environmental factors → bilateral arrest before diaphragm closure. LV hypoplasia: from ↓ pulmonary venous return + RV compression → ↑ LA/PV pressure → pulmonary venous HTN → ↓ iNO response early. Assess: echo LV size, function, mitral inflow, pulmonary venous return. If LV dysfunction: milrinone + consider DA open as LV pop-off. Bosentan + sildenafil: for persistent/chronic CDH PH.
Q: SSRI ใน maternal depression ควรหยุดยาในไตรมาส 3 หรือไม่?

ตอบ: ไม่แนะนำให้หยุดยา routine. Chambers NEJM 2006: SSRI late pregnancy → ↑ PPHN risk แต่ absolute risk modest. Huybrechts JAMA 2015: when controlling for maternal depression, risk significantly attenuated. Risk of untreated maternal depression: ↑ preterm birth, ↓ prenatal care, postpartum depression/suicide. Mechanism SSRI: ↑ 5-HT → 5-HT2A+ROCK → ↑ PVR + ductal constriction. Practice: ไม่ต้อง stop SSRI — แต่ monitor ทารกสำหรับ PPHN อย่างใกล้ชิด + ประวัติ SSRI ต้องอยู่ใน PPHN workup.
Q: ACD คืออะไร? คิดถึงเมื่อไหร่? confirm ด้วยอะไร?

ตอบ: ACD (alveolar capillary dysplasia with misalignment of PV): rare, universally fatal neonatal condition. Histology: alveolar simplification + remodeled arterioles + "misaligned" pulmonary veins (bronchopulmonary anastomoses) in same sheath as artery+bronchus, bypassing alveolar capillary bed. คิดถึงเมื่อ: (1) term, (2) severe refractory PPHN not responding to iNO + sildenafil + ECMO, (3) ± GI/GU/CV anomalies, (4) transient improvement then relapse. Genetics: FOXF1 mutation/deletion ใน 40%. Confirm: open lung biopsy (gold standard). No survivors without lung transplant.
Q: Placental insufficiency connect กับ BPD-associated PH ได้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Placental MVU (maternal vascular underperfusion) → ↓ villous vascularity → ↓ placental VEGF-A + PlGF in cord blood → predicts subsequent BPD-PH (Mestan J Pediatr 2017). Mechanism: failed placental angiogenesis = global vascular signaling defects → intrauterine vascular programming → postnatal pulmonary vascular disease. FGR + absent end-diastolic flow → in utero PH → postnatal vascular dysfunction. Practice: ส่ง placenta pathology ใน FGR + preterm; cord blood angiogenic biomarkers ช่วย stratify PH risk. Anticipate PH workup ตั้งแต่แรกรับใน NICU.
Q: อธิบาย mechanism ของ 100% O2 resuscitation ที่เพิ่ม risk ของ PPHN

ตอบ: 100% O2 → ROS (hydrogen peroxide, superoxide, peroxynitrite) จาก immature antioxidant defense + NADPH oxidase. ROS → (1) ↓ eNOS expression + activity; (2) ↑ PDE5 activity (via mitochondrial ROS) → ↓ cGMP; (3) oxidizes sGC → NO-insensitive form; (4) ↑ prolyl hydroxylase → ↓ HIF → ↓ VEGF. Net: vasoconstriction + vascular remodeling. Lakshminrusimha 2007: 100% vs 21% O2 resuscitation — PVR falls similarly within minutes, but 100% O2 → impaired subsequent iNO relaxation. NRP 2021: start 21% O2 (preterm) or room air (term); titrate SpO2 to target range.
NL Day 04 Preview

The Newborn Lung Ch.4 — The 'Omics' of the New Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia
พรุ่งนี้จะเรียน Chapter 4: The 'Omics' of the New BPD — หลังเข้าใจ vascular development biology วันนี้แล้ว เราจะมาดูว่า genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, และ metabolomics เปลี่ยนความเข้าใจเราเกี่ยวกับ BPD อย่างไร. เนื้อหา: genetic susceptibility loci (SPOCK2, VEGF, MBL2), epigenetic changes (DNA methylation, histone modification ใน preterm lung), miRNA signatures, exhaled breath condensate metabolomics เป็น BPD biomarker. เชื่อมกับวันนี้: VEGF + HIF pathways ที่เรียนจะปรากฏใน genomics/proteomics data ของ BPD patients.
